name: Blood Circulation Process
description: Blood Circulation Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A42[Heart Contraction] --> B42[Ventricle Ejection]
    B42 --> C42[Arterial Flow]
    C42 --> D42[Capillary Exchange]
    D42 --> E42[O2 Delivery]
    D42 --> F42[CO2 Removal]
    D42 --> G42[Nutrient Delivery]
    E42 --> H42[Venous Return]
    F42 --> H42
    G42 --> H42
    H42 --> I42[Atrial Filling]
    I42 --> A42
    
    style A42 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B42 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C42 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D42 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E42 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F42 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G42 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H42 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I42 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Blood Circulation Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: The cardiovascular system: development, disease, and regeneration
Cardiac output and its regulation
sources.authors: Guyton, AC.; Coleman, TG.; Granger, HJ.

sources.title: Endothelial cells and the regulation of microvascular exchange
sources.authors: Siflinger-Birnboim, A.; Goligorsky, MS.; Lum, H.; Malik, AB.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
